findings.signature: database.exec() DDL: CREATE TABLE memoization_records + dependency_edges + indexes
findings.notes: database.exec() runs multi-statement DDL; Turso lacks loadExtension and exec() multi-statement batching may behave differently.

findings.signature: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET (3-column composite PK)
findings.notes: Standard upsert; Turso supports ON CONFLICT but datetime() default expressions need verification.

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE dependents(child_path) AS (SELECT ... UNION SELECT ... JOIN dependents) ORDER BY
findings.notes: Recursive CTE for dependency traversal; Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be rewritten as iterative application-side loop.

findings.signature: INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependency_edges with cycle detection guard
findings.notes: INSERT OR IGNORE is supported by Turso; cycle detection calls collectDependents which uses recursive CTE.

findings.signature: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS + ensureTemporalColumns(database)
findings.notes: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS is Turso-compatible; ensureTemporalColumns() needs separate audit for DDL surface.

findings.signature: database.transaction(() => { SELECT + UPSERT + logWeightChange })
findings.notes: Explicit better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper; Turso transactions are async (await tx()). Entire function must become async.

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { for edge: insertEdge() }) with cache invalidation
findings.notes: Nested transaction (calls insertEdge which also wraps in transaction); better-sqlite3 supports savepoints but Turso transaction API differs.

findings.signature: database.transaction(() => { PRAGMA table_info + INSERT OR IGNORE loop })
findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info() inside transaction for dynamic column discovery; Turso PRAGMA support is limited — may need schema caching.

findings.signature: database.transaction(() => { SELECT strength + UPDATE + logWeightChange })
findings.notes: Synchronous transaction wrapping read-modify-write for atomicity; must become async for Turso.

findings.signature: database.transaction(() => { SELECT current + SELECT history + UPDATE + logWeightChange })
findings.notes: Multi-statement transaction with fallback SELECT chain; synchronous transaction wrapper blocks Turso migration.

findings.signature: UPDATE causal_edges SET last_accessed = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?
findings.notes: Simple UPDATE; Turso-compatible but datetime() function behavior needs verification.

findings.signature: INSERT INTO weight_history (edge_id, old_strength, new_strength, changed_by, reason)
findings.notes: Simple INSERT; Turso-compatible. No transaction wrapper — relies on caller's transaction context.

findings.signature: runInTransaction(db, () => { sweepCausalEdges() })
findings.notes: Uses runInTransaction wrapper (from transaction-manager.ts) — that wrapper must become async for Turso.

findings.signature: database.exec() DDL: CREATE TABLE causal_edge_tombstones + 3 indexes
findings.notes: Multi-statement DDL via database.exec(); Turso exec() may not support multi-statement batching — must split.

findings.signature: runInTransaction(database, () => { ensureSchema + readEdges + INSERT tombstones + DELETE edges })
findings.notes: Core tombstone lifecycle: read edges → insert tombstones → delete edges in single transaction. Uses runInTransaction which must become async.

findings.signature: DELETE FROM causal_edges WHERE id = ? (per-id loop)
findings.notes: Per-row DELETE loop inside transaction; could be batched but Turso supports parameterized DELETE.

findings.signature: causalEdges.init() + cleanupStaleGeneratedEdges() + causalEdges.insertEdge() loop
findings.notes: Orchestrates multiple write calls (sweep + insert) without wrapping in own transaction; relies on per-call transactionality.

findings.signature: SELECT stale edges + sweepCausalEdges(database, { edgeIds: staleIds })
findings.notes: Reads stale edges then delegates to sweep for tombstoning+deletion; two-phase read-then-write pattern needs atomicity guarantee.

findings.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS ... maxHops
findings.notes: Core recursive CTE for graph traversal with bidirectional edges and relation-weighted scoring. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS in application code.

findings.signature: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM causal_edges WHERE relation=? AND datetime(extracted_at) >= datetime(?)
findings.notes: Rate-limiting query using datetime() comparison; Turso supports datetime() but behavior edge cases differ.
